name: Andalusia (Spain)

colors.symbolism: hope and the colour of the Umayyads of Cordoba; in Infante's account, also strength
peace, and the colour of the Almohads

outlines of the coat of arms

description: Three equal horizontal stripes of green, white, and green, with the coat of arms of Andalusia centred on the white stripe.
history: Designed by Blas Infante in 1918 and adopted by the Assembly of Ronda in 1933; recognised in the Statute of Autonomy of Andalusia in 1981 and confirmed by Law 3/1982 of 21 December.

features.symbolism: The white-between-green design (the Arbonaida) was associated by Andalusian nationalist Blas Infante with the colours of the Almohad and Umayyad dynasties that once ruled al-Andalus.

features.role: coat of arms of Andalusia (Hercules between the two Pillars of Hercules, taming two lions, with the motto Andalucia por si, para Espana y la Humanidad)
features.symbolism: Hercules, the Pillars at Gibraltar and Ceuta, and two tamed lions evoke the legendary founding of Cadiz and the strength of the Andalusian people; the motto means 'Andalusia for itself, for Spain and Humanity'.
